data.title: ClearBridge Emerging Markets Strategy adds GFI and MNTHF; exits Shenzhen Mindray Bio-Medical Electronics
data.description:
• The ClearBridge Emerging Markets Strategy (MCEIX) underperformed its MSCI Emerging Markets benchmark in Q1 2026.
  • During Q1 2026, the fund initiated new positions in Gold Fields (NYSE: GFI), Minth Group (MNTHF), and Samsung Heavy Industries.
  • In Q1 2026, the fund exited Shenzhen Mindray Bio-Medical Electronics.
  • Source.

data.title: Lockheed Martin expands venture fund to $1B to back defense tech
data.description:
Lockheed Martin (LMT) on Tuesday said it will expand its corporate venture capital arm, Lockheed Martin Ventures, to $1 billion, more than doubling the fund’s previous $400 million capacity in what the company described as its largest increase since the unit was established in 2007.

The additional capital is expected to be deployed over time to support the development of technologies tied to national security, with a focus on advancing early-stage innovations toward operational use within the defense industrial base.

Chief Financial Officer Evan Scott said the venture arm plays a role in identifying and developing emerging technologies that can be integrated into the company’s broader portfolio. He said the investments are intended to help build a pipeline of technologies relevant to future defense needs.

Lockheed Martin Ventures has invested more than $500 million across over 120 companies since its inception, according to the company. More than 60 of those firms have gone on to become suppliers, collectively receiving more than $750 million in contracts from Lockheed Martin.

The fund has targeted areas such as quantum computing, artificial intelligence, autonomy, directed energy, advanced materials and microelectronics. Over the past two years, the company added 25 firms to its portfolio.

The venture unit was launched with $100 million in initial funding and has operated continuously for nearly two decades, making it one of the longer-running corporate venture funds in the aerospace and defense sector.

Lockheed Martin (LMT) said it plans to continue using the fund to identify technologies that could transition into its supply chain and broader defense programs. The company is also preparing to host its annual Demo Day in August at its headquarters, where portfolio companies and startups will present their work to company executives and technical teams.

data.title: STAK regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• STAK (NASDAQ:STAK)  announced on Tuesday it had received a Nasdaq letter confirming it has regained compliance with the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement under Listing Rule 5550(a)(2).
  • The company said the deficiency, noted in October 2025 after shares traded below $1.00 for 30 consecutive days, was regained after the stock closed at or above $1.00 for 10 straight sessions from March 27 to April 10, 2026.

data.title: Amazon to acquire Globalstar; inks deal with Apple to provide satellite services for iPhone, Watch
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) announced today it plans to acquire Globalstar (GSAT) satellites, which allows Amazon Leo to add direct-to-device services with its low Earth orbit network.

In addition, Amazon also inked a deal with Apple (AAPL) to enable Leo satellite services for iPhones and Apple Watches, allowing users to text emergency services, message friends and family, and request roadside assistance when they are beyond the reach of terrestrial cellular networks.

Globalstar shares had surged 9% during pre-market trading on Tuesday.

data.title: PTX Metals increases size of private placement to $5.5M
data.description:
• PTX Metals (PTX:CA) announced on Tuesday that it has increased the size of its previously announced non-brokered private placements from up to $3M to up to $5.5M.
  • As set forth in its earlier press release, the company proposes to complete both an offering of flow-through common shares (“FT Shares”) at $0.125 per FT share and units of the company at $0.11 per unit.
  • Each unit will consist of one common share and one-half of one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant is exercisable to acquire one additional common share at $0.18 for a period of 36 months from the date of issuance.
  • The company said that the warrants will be subject to an acceleration provision, whereby the company may accelerate the expiry date of the warrants if the closing price of the company's common shares on the TSX Venture Exchange is at or above $0.40 for more than 20 consecutive trading days, in accordance with the terms of the warrants.
  • The company had initially proposed to issue up to $1.5M of both FT shares and units for total gross proceeds of $3M and now intends to issue up to $3.5M of FT shares and $2M of units for total gross proceeds of $5.5M.
  • The company intends to use the proceeds from the issuance of the units for general corporate expenses and working capital purposes and to use the gross proceeds from the issuance of the FT shares to incur eligible "Canadian exploration expenses." 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GameSquare expands share repurchase authorization to $15M
data.description:
• GameSquare announced on Tuesday that the company's board of directors has authorized an increase in its stock repurchase program from $5M to $15M.
  • Under the repurchase program, GameSquare may purchase shares of its common stock on a discretionary basis from time to time through open market repurchases, in privately negotiated transactions, or other means.
  • As of March 31, 2026, GameSquare has repurchased 5.06M shares of its common stock for $2.5M, representing an average repurchase price of approximately $0.49 since the company's initial repurchase program started in October 2025. The company has approximately $12.5M remaining under its current authorization.
  • GAME -0.68% premarket to $0.3654.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Momentus announces pricing of $5M private placement of common stock
data.description:
• Momentus (MNTS) announced on Tuesday that it has entered into a securities purchase agreement with a new fundamental institutional investor for the purchase and sale of 1.33M shares of its common stock (or common stock equivalents in lieu thereof) at $3.75 per share in a private placement priced at-the-market under Nasdaq rules.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $5M, before deducting placement agent fees and other estimated offering expenses.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about April 15, 2026.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes.
  • MNTS +5.89% premarket to $5.21.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Core AI and Allianca Group form strategic joint venture
data.description:
• Core AI (CHAI) announced on Tuesday the formation of a strategic joint venture with Allianca Group. 
  • The joint venture’s objective is to position both organizations at the forefront of the global race to deploy AI-ready digital infrastructure capable of supporting high-performance computing and artificial intelligence workloads at enterprise and hyperscale levels, the company said. 
  • CHAI shares gained 16.67% to $1.26 during premarket trading on Tuesday.
  • Core AI will contribute its AI-native infrastructure strategy and capital markets expertise. Allianca will contribute a fully integrated, turnkey delivery model and direct contributions to the delivery of more than 720 MW of mission-critical data center capacity globally.
  • Together, the joint venture is designed to compress development timelines, reduce execution risk, and deliver AI-ready capacity faster than traditional build cycles allow.
  • The joint venture intends to operationalize a fully integrated infrastructure platform spanning the complete development lifecycle: power-aware site selection and strategy, preconstruction planning and program controls, modular deployment methodologies, construction execution, utility coordination, and commissioning. This end-to-end ownership model eliminates the fragmentation that often slows conventional infrastructure development and gives owners and capital partners a single accountable delivery partner from site identification through power-on.
  • The joint venture plans to identify and target high-priority AI infrastructure markets where the convergence of power availability, permitting environments, fiber connectivity, and construction readiness creates near-term deployment opportunities.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AGF Management Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.30, adjusted revenue of C$95.3M
data.description:
• AGF Management press release (AGFMF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.30.
  • Revenue of C$95.3M (-14.5% Y/Y).
  • AGF reported total assets under management and fee-earning assets1 of $60.5 billion compared to $60.4 billion as at November 30, 2025 and $53.8 billion as at February 28, 2025. AGF generated strong free cash flows of $36.0 million in the quarter, up 14% from the prior year.

data.title: Citigroup GAAP EPS of $3.06 beats by $0.43, revenue of $24.63B beats by $1.04B
data.description:
• Citigroup press release (C): Q1 GAAP EPS of $3.06 beats by $0.43.
  • Revenue of $24.63B (+14.0% Y/Y) beats by $1.04B.
  • Revenues increased 14% from the prior-year period, driven by growth in each ofCiti’s five interconnected businesses and Legacy Franchises in All Other, as wellas the impact of foreign exchange translation, partially offset by a decline inCorporate/Other, also in All Other.
  • Citigroup provision for credit losses was $2.8 billion, reflecting $2.2 billion of net credit losses and a net allowance for credit losses (ACL) build of $597 million, driven by portfolio quality, including seasonal mix changes, as well as increased uncertainty in the macroeconomic outlook, partially offset by refinements to loss assumptions and lower net lending activity. Net credit losses were down 10% from the prior-year period, driven by decreases in USCC and Markets, partially offset by an increase in Legacy Franchises. The provision in the prioryear period was $2.7 billion, reflecting $2.5 billion of net credit losses and a net ACL build of $264 million, driven by increased uncertainty in the macroeconomic outlook and portfolio quality, largely offset by lower net lending activity.
  • Citigroup’s book value per share of $112.22 at quarter end increased 8% versus the prior-year period, and tangible book value per share of $99.01 at quarter end also increased 8% versus the prior-year period.
  • Shares +0.4% PM.
